mission.title: Install and Configure LiteLLM
mission.description: LiteLLM is an AI Gateway that allows you to call over 100 LLM APIs in a unified OpenAI format. It provides features like cost tracking, load balancing, and logging, making it ideal for applications that require integration with multiple AI models. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration and management of API keys for different LLM providers.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the LiteLLM CLI tool to manage the deployment and configuration.

```bash

pip install litellm==1.81.14

```

> Install the specific version of LiteLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure kubectl to use the new cluster's credentials.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command fetches the credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in the cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Deploy LiteLLM
steps.description:
Deploy the LiteLLM application to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.81.14/deploy/litellm-deployment.yaml

```

> This command deploys LiteLLM using the provided YAML configuration.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for the LiteLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment litellm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.81.14/deploy/litellm-monitoring.yaml

```

> This command enables autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Deploy monitoring configurations.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LiteLLM
uninstall.description:
Remove the LiteLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the deployment.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.81.14/deploy/litellm-deployment.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete litellm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command removes the LiteLLM deployment.
> This command deletes the entire Kubernetes cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LiteLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your configurations and data. This step ensures that you can restore your setup if needed.

```bash

kubectl get all -n litellm > litellm-backup.yaml

kubectl set image deployment/litellm litellm=registry/image:tag

kubectl rollout status deployment/litellm

```

> Backup current configurations.
> Upgrade to the new version of LiteLLM.
> Verify that the upgrade was successful.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or is incorrectly tagged.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LiteLLM service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the deployment with 'kubectl get pods' and ensure the service is correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** API keys for LLM providers are not set or are incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify that the correct API keys are set in the environment variables.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new LLMs including Bedrock and improved logging features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for older API versions.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your API keys and configurations to match the new LLM endpoints.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods' showing the LiteLLM pod running with a status of 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
